title: andropogon schottii subvar. typicus
intro: Andropogon schottii subvar. typicus, also known as a variety of the Schott's Bluestem plant, is a type of grass native to the southwestern United States. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Andropogon schottii subvar. typicus can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
